# emu/win95 — QEMU assets

Everything in this directory except this README is **git-ignored**. Drop your
own files here; `tools/setup_win95.sh` will look for them and wire up a qcow2
overlay so iteration never writes to your original image.

## Required files

| File | What it is | Where to get it |
|---|---|---|
| `base.img` | raw Windows 95 HDD image (TCP/IP + Winsock installed, NE2000 at 0x300/IRQ10) | your own install; setup copies from `WIN95_IMG` (set in `.env`) or arg 1 |

## Generated by setup / each run

| File | What it is |
|---|---|
| `hda.qcow2` | copy-on-write overlay on `base.img` — delete to revert the guest |
| `relic.iso` | ISO9660 CD image, repacked on every `make run-win95` with the fresh `RELIC.EXE` + a `RELIC.CFG` carrying your host `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` |

## Workflow

```sh
make setup-win95 IMG=~/path/to/windows95.img   # one-time (or set WIN95_IMG in .env)
make run-win95                  # build + pack + boot, every time
make run-win95 ARGS=--no-build  # just relaunch
make run-win95 ARGS=--fresh     # drop overlay, clean OS
```

Inside the guest: open **D:\\** and run `RELIC.EXE` (or `RELIC -p "hello"`
from a DOS prompt).
